novel_features.name: Pool readiness composite
novel_features.description: One-shot "is the pool ready for guests?" view: chemistry in range, temp at setpoint, no active alarms, pump running, with a traffic-light verdict.
novel_features.rationale: Compounds telemetry + alarms + MSP config + setpoints in a single call; the Hayward app makes you tap through four screens to assemble the same answer.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this when an agent or user wants a one-shot pool-state summary instead of correlating three commands.

novel_features.name: Ready-by ETA
novel_features.description: Enables the heater and computes when to start it so the pool hits your target temperature by a specified arrival time, using the learned heat rate from telemetry history.
novel_features.rationale: Heat-rate learning (°F/hr per body of water) requires telemetry deltas across days; the cloud and app have no ETA math.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this instead of "set heater + setpoint and guess" when the user has a specific swim time.

novel_features.name: Historical chemistry export
novel_features.description: Weekly/monthly pH, ORP, salt, and temperature history from the local store, exportable as CSV or JSON for HOA / service / insurance records.
novel_features.rationale: The cloud returns "now" only; users today roll their own InfluxDB to get any chemistry history at all.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this when an agent needs trend data or a compliance log over a date range.

novel_features.name: Chemistry drift + forecast
novel_features.description: Detects pH/ORP/salt drift versus a rolling baseline before Hayward's static thresholds fire; --forecast projects when each metric will leave the safe range.
novel_features.rationale: Requires append-only time-series and slope math; cloud and app only fire on absolute thresholds.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this when the user wants early warning, not just "alarm is active."

novel_features.name: Equipment runtime totals
novel_features.description: Pump hours, heater hours, salt-cell hours derived from telemetry deltas — for maintenance planning, warranty, and end-of-season service.
novel_features.rationale: Cumulative runtime isn't a cloud API field; only delta accumulation against the local time-series can yield it.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this when a service-business agent needs maintenance projections or a warranty case-file.

novel_features.name: Command audit + replay
novel_features.description: Every Set* command issued via this CLI is logged with who/when/what/result. --replay <id> re-issues a prior command for quick undo or redo.
novel_features.rationale: Local audit trail; the cloud doesn't expose per-user command history.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this when an agent or operator needs to know what was changed recently or roll back a misfire.

novel_features.name: Why isn't this running?
novel_features.description: Diagnose why a pump, heater, or light isn't running: correlates active alarms, current relay state, scheduled run windows, heater demand, and superchlor lockouts into one explanation.
novel_features.rationale: Joins four cloud calls + a versioned schedule snapshot the Hayward UI never surfaces; no wrapper packages this today.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this when an agent is asked to triage "X isn't running" instead of telling the user to open the app.

novel_features.name: Schedule change detection
novel_features.description: Diffs today's MSP-config schedule tree against prior versioned snapshots; catches silent edits made by service techs or by other app users.
novel_features.rationale: Requires versioned msp_config_snapshots; the cloud has no schedule-history endpoint.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this when a user reports unexpected pool behavior and you need to know what changed.

novel_features.name: Multi-site morning sweep
novel_features.description: Across every site in the account, surface active alarms, out-of-range chemistry, and offline controllers in a single report — built for pool-service businesses doing route planning.
novel_features.rationale: Pool-service ops today open the Hayward app per-site; only a CLI with the stored site list and parallel calls collapses the morning routine.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this when an agent is asked to prioritize the day's truck rolls across many pools.

narrative.headline: Take control of every Hayward OmniLogic feature, plus a local store, schedule diffs, chemistry trends, and a morning multi-site sweep no other tool offers.
narrative.value_prop: Drives the same partner API the Hayward mobile app and Home Assistant integration use, but adds the historical chemistry log, the equipment diagnostic the cloud refuses to compute, the schedule-change detector for service techs, and the multi-site alarm sweep pool-service businesses ask for. Agent-native JSON throughout, with a local SQLite store that turns single-shot cloud reads into compound answers.
narrative.auth_narrative: OmniLogic uses a two-stage auth: a JSON login against services-gamma.haywardcloud.net returns a token + refresh token, then every operation POSTs an XML envelope to the legacy HAAPI endpoint with the token in the header. The CLI caches the token under your XDG state directory and refreshes it transparently. Set HAYWARD_USER (your account email) and HAYWARD_PW; the first call to any command logs in automatically.

quickstart.comment: Verify auth + reachability + token cache before anything else.
Pull sites, equipment inventory, current telemetry, and alarms into the local store.
One-shot pool-readiness composite — chemistry, temp, alarms, pump state with a traffic-light verdict.
Export the last week's pH/ORP/salt/temp readings for service records.
Enable the heater and compute when to start it so the pool reaches 84°F by 6pm.

troubleshoots.fix: Confirm HAYWARD_USER is the email you log in with at haywardomnilogic.com (post-Oct-2025 the API rejects username-only); rerun `doctor` to re-cache the token.

troubleshoots.symptom: Token works for a day then fails
troubleshoots.fix: Token cache TTL is 24h; the CLI auto-refreshes via /auth-service/v2/refresh. If refresh fails (e.g., laptop slept past expiry), delete `~/.local/state/hayward-omnilogic/auth.json` and rerun any command.

troubleshoots.symptom: Schedule diff says "no baseline" on first run
troubleshoots.fix: Run `sync --full` at least once so the CLI has a prior MSP-config snapshot to compare against.
telemetry samples are appended by `sync` (or any read-side call); after `sync --full` you have one point. Run `sync` periodically (cron / launchd / 30-60s minimum interval per community guidance).

narrative.when_to_use: Pick this CLI when an agent needs to read or control a Hayward OmniLogic pool/spa system over the partner cloud API: pool owners managing one pool, integrators wiring OmniLogic into agentic workflows, and pool-service businesses managing many sites. The CLI replaces ad-hoc Python scripts on top of `omnilogic-api`, the Hayward mobile app for diagnostics and trends, and the per-site click-through pool-service operators do every morning.

recipes.title: Is the pool ready for guests?
recipes.explanation: One call, structured answer. Use --select to keep the JSON tight when piping to an LLM.

recipes.title: Weekly chemistry log for the service record
recipes.explanation: Cron this every Sunday and the binder writes itself.

recipes.title: Why isn't the main pump running right now?
recipes.explanation: Returns the explanation as structured fields: active_alarms, scheduled_window, current_state, heater_demand, superchlor_lockout.

recipes.title: Morning sweep across every site
recipes.command: hayward-omnilogic-pp-cli sweep --alarms --chemistry --json | jq '.sites[] | select(.alarms != [] or .chemistry_out_of_range == true)'
recipes.explanation: Pool-service operators: this collapses 12 in-app site-checks into one piped command.

recipes.title: Preheat for an evening swim with ETA from learned heat rate
recipes.explanation: Pass --dry-run first to see when the heater would start; drop the flag to actually enable it.

trigger_phrases: show me the pool chemistry log
why isn't the pool pump running
